Discord .well-known Domain Verification Doesn't Work on GitHub Pages — Here's the Fix
I wanted to set up Discord's domain verification on my blog recently and hit a snag, so I'm writing it down.
Discord offers two verification methods — DNS or HTTPS. I went with HTTPS, which means Discord wants a specific file at:
language-txt shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
My blog runs on Hexo, so I created the file like this:
language-bash shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

cd hexo-blog
mkdir source/.well-known
touch source/.well-known/discord
And dropped Discord's verification content into the file:
Problem one — Hexo ignores files and folders prefixed with
by default. So you can't just create
; Hexo skips it during build, and Discord can't verify your domain.
Fix that with a small change to
language-yml shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

# Include / Exclude file(s)
## include:/exclude: options only apply to the 'source/' folder
include:
exclude:
ignore:
## include:/exclude: options only apply to the 'source/' folder
You might excitedly hit "Verify Domain" on Discord at this point — and find it still doesn't work. Even your browser can't reach
, so the file isn't actually being served.
The fix is to create a separate GitHub repo named exactly
, then put the verification file inside it:
Then go into Settings → Pages and enable GitHub Pages for that repo so it can serve:
After enabling, it should look like this:
Visit your domain again and you can reach
— actually it downloads as a file. Now hit Discord's "Verify Domain" and it works:
Here's the verification repo for reference:

I tried to set up Discord's HTTPS domain verification on my GitHub Pages-hosted Hexo blog and hit a wall — GitHub Pages flat-out ignores .well-known directories. This post walks through what went wrong, why Hexo also needed a tweak to even generate the .well-known folder, and the workaround that actually got Discord to verify: spinning up a dedicated .well-known repo as a separate GitHub Pages site.
